messages.content: Need to look at our budget numbers again. The kitchen reno plus the new sitter is really hitting us hard.
I can help you review your budget. What specific changes are you seeing in your monthly expenses?
Our monthly savings dropped to $400. Used to be putting away $1,800 back in October before all this started.
That's a significant drop. What are the main drivers behind the $1,400 reduction?

Well, we hired the after-school sitter this week - $320 per week for Lily and Ethan since I'm swamped at work. Plus our food budget is up 40% because we're eating out constantly during the renovation.
The sitter cost alone is about $1,280 monthly, which explains most of the difference. How long do you expect these elevated expenses to continue?
Kitchen should be done by March, so the food situation will improve. The sitter might be longer term though - depends how the promotion track goes. At least my bonus is expected in February.
That February bonus should provide some relief. Are you planning to use it to offset these temporary expenses or put it toward the renovation costs?
Probably split it. We already paid the 30% deposit to Ramirez Bros, but we'll need more for the final payment. Just trying to keep our heads above water until things settle down.
It sounds like you have a clear timeline for when some of these costs will decrease. Would it help to map out a month-by-month projection through March?
Yeah, that would be good. Need to make sure we can still cover everything without dipping into the kids' college funds.

summary: Diane reviews her strained budget, noting monthly savings dropped to $400 due to new $320/week sitter costs and increased food expenses during kitchen renovation. She discusses timeline for relief and plans for February bonus allocation.
